summary: Merged continuation of Corneria stage decomp work from #2381. The PR focused on grcorneria Arwing spawning/attachment/projectile behavior, added typed Ground/Arwing overlays, and improved matching across many Corneria functions. decomp.dev reported +4492 matched bytes, 8 new 100% matches, and major partial progress on grCorneria_801DED50 and grCorneria_801DCE1C.

key_files.role: Main Corneria stage implementation. Added/reworked Arwing scheduling, Arwing child-object initialization/update, projectile/item spawning, background/camera helper matching, assertions, and smash-taunt/dialog setup.
Ground/stage layout fixes. Added grCorneria_GroundVars2 overlay, changed grArwing_GroundVars xE0/xE4/xE8 into a Vec3, and retyped several Corneria fields.

what_changed: Added includes for it/items/itarwinglaser.h and pl/player.h, supporting calls such as it_802E72E0, it_802E7654, Player_GetPlayerSlotType, and Player_LoadPlayerCoords.
Retyped grCn_StageData fields x1C, x20, x28, x2C, and x68 from s32 to f32 where the stage data is used as floating-point configuration.
Adjusted grCn_Data layout by flattening x3D8 from s32[9][3] to s32[27] and exposing x478[3] before the remaining padding.

Added grCorneria_801DCE1C in C as the Arwing spawn scheduler: it handles stage 0x46 suppression, normal versus special grCn_804D69B0 modes, arwing_gobj slot counts, timers, unique type selection, far/near checks, random group selection, and grCn_SpawnArwing calls.

Replaced the no-match marker for grCorneria_801DED50 with a large partial C implementation covering Arwing-linked child object updates, animations, position derivation from Arwing/ship joints, firing cadence, blast-zone checks, angle normalization, audio cues, Arwing laser item spawning, Great Fox laser calls, and cleanup when the parent Arwing disappears.

Matched/reworked grCorneria_801DE8E4 and grCorneria_801DF8D0 around the new gv.corneria2 overlay, including type switches for arwing_type 1-9 versus 10-13, child object creation/copying, scale setup, flags, timers, and sentinel pointer fields.
Changed grArwing_GroundVars so the former xE0/xE4/xE8 scalar floats are represented as Vec3 xE0, then updated Arwing code to use xE0.x/y/z and pass &xE0 to HSD_JObjSetTranslate.
Added struct grCorneria_GroundVars2 to the Ground gv union, documented as Arwing slot ground vars for callbacks 2 and 10, with pointer/integer fields overlapping grCorneria_GroundVars.

Improved smaller matching sites in grCorneria_801DCCFC, grCorneria_801DDAC4, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801DE024, grCorneria_801DE568, grCorneria_801E03C8, grCorneria_801E08CC, grCorneria_801E0A74, grCorneria_801E0E40, grCorneria_801E1060, grCorneria_801E1348, grCorneria_801E1970, grCorneria_801E1BF0, grCorneria_801E2228, and grCorneria_801E25C4.

decomp.dev reported new 100% matches for grCorneria_801DE8E4, grCorneria_801DF8D0, grCorneria_801DDE88, grCorneria_801E08CC, grCorneria_801E1970, grCorneria_801E1060, grCorneria_801DCCFC, and grCorneria_801E2228.
smart_moves: Introduced grCorneria_GroundVars2 instead of continuing to force Arwing-slot state through grCorneria_GroundVars casts; this made pointer fields, counters, and slot IDs explicit while preserving the union overlay.
Grouped Arwing translation scratch fields into Vec3 xE0, enabling natural HSD_JObjSetTranslate usage and cleaner ABS/angle logic in grCorneria_801DE568.
Used a local grCn_Data* data = &grCn_803E1D38 in several functions to simplify repeated global accesses and improve generated code shape.

Added Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX with direct translate.x assignment and dirty-matrix handling, then used it in grCorneria_801E1060 to match codegen where the normal HSD_JObjSetTranslateX call did not.

Preserved codegen-sensitive details such as PAD_STACK sizes, switch versus if/else choices, break versus return in switch bodies, explicit temporaries, signed casts, HSD_ASSERT/HSD_ASSERTREPORT macros, and __FILE__ in OSReport.
Converted opaque byte-pointer manipulation in grCorneria_801E25C4 to a typed grSmashTaunt_GroundVars pointer and dialog_joints indexing, improving readability while still improving match percentage.
decomp_lessons: When a Ground gv union region is reused by different callbacks, add a second overlay struct with offset comments rather than abusing unrelated fields; PR 2383’s grCorneria_GroundVars2 is the concrete pattern.
If assembly indexes a data table linearly, a flat array such as x3D8[27] may match better than a guessed multidimensional declaration like x3D8[9][3].
Do not leave unnamed config fields as s32 just because names are unknown; retype x-fields to f32 when arithmetic and callsites show float semantics.
Accessor spelling matters for matching: this PR mixes GET_GROUND, gobj->user_data, HSD_GObjGetUserData, GET_JOBJ, gobj->hsd_obj, and HSD_GObjGetHSDObj depending on the function’s needed codegen.

For stubborn stage functions, small source-shape changes can be decisive: condition order, assignment expressions inside switches, explicit modulo expansion, temporary locals for constants, separate HSD_Randf calls, and M_TAU/ABS macros all appeared in matching improvements.
Large partial decomp work can still be valuable when tied to real struct/header improvements; grCorneria_801DED50 moved from 0.00% to 66.01% while exposing reusable Arwing-state layout.
naming_conventions: Existing Corneria globals retained the grCn_ prefix and address-style names, such as grCn_803E1D38, grCn_804D69A0, grCn_804D69A8, and grCn_804D69B0.
Small file-local helpers use the grCn_ prefix plus descriptive action names: grCn_RandRange, grCn_CheckFar, grCn_SpawnArwing, and grCn_PickUniqueType.
The new overlay type is named grCorneria_GroundVars2 and keeps offset-style fields xC4, xC8, xDC, xF0, etc. because semantics are still mostly unknown.
The Arwing ground vars keep offset naming but collapse adjacent position fields into Vec3 xE0 once their vector use is evidenced.
Header/type changes favor exact signedness where evidenced: grCorneria_GroundVars x10C/x110 became s32 and x118 became s8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(24) to reproduce stack layout in specific functions.
Replaced normal HSD_JObjSetTranslateX calls with Fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslateX in grCorneria_801E1060 to match inline behavior and dirty-flag side effects.

Reshaped control flow for codegen: grCorneria_801DCCFC changed an if/else to a switch with grCn_804D69B0 = (imax = 0); grCorneria_801DDE88 used break instead of return; grCorneria_801E03C8 changed a switch to if/else.
Used ABS and M_TAU for angle normalization instead of manual sign flips and 2.0 * M_PI expressions.
Changed assertions/reporting to HSD_ASSERT, HSD_ASSERTREPORT, and OSReport with __FILE__ where that matched better than literal file strings or raw __assert calls.
Inserted signed casts and signed field types, such as s32 rand_id and (s8) gp->gv.corneria.x119, to avoid mismatched unsigned codegen.
Used direct gobj field access in some matched functions, e.g. gobj->user_data and gobj->hsd_obj, rather than always using macros.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in this PR slice.
The PR body only says: "Draft PR, continuing from #2381"; intent beyond continuing Corneria work is not explicitly documented.
decomp.dev reported 62.18% total matched code, +0.12% overall, +4492 matched bytes, 8 new matches, 10 improvements in unmatched items, and one .sdata regression of -4 bytes.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2381 for the preceding grcorneria scaffolding that this PR says it continues.
Search later PRs for grCorneria_801DED50 and grCorneria_801DCE1C to see whether the remaining unmatched percentages were completed.
Search for grCorneria_GroundVars2 or other GroundVars2 overlays to reuse the union-overlay typing pattern in stage decomp.
Investigate the decomp.dev .sdata regression from this PR if future work touches nearby constants or static data ordering.
Audit grCn_StageData x1C/x20/x28/x2C/x68 and grCn_Data x3D8/x478 usages for possible semantic field names.
